summary:
The main reason for speech issues in individuals with cleft palates is difficulty separating air flow through proper closure at the velum, affecting sound production. This directly relates to impaired function of the soft palate which controls this process. \boxed{B}
Speech challenges in people born with cleft palates stem mainly from their anatomy causing poor control during speaking; specifically, they struggle with isolating breath passage so it doesn’t mix into or escape via nose instead of just vocal tract as intended. Thus pinpointing impairment related to inadequate soft-palate movement stands out among provided choices.\boxed{B}
